_note_revenue: Delaying commissioning a 60 MW data center costs an estimated $14.2 million per month in lost revenue. Source: CONSTRUCTION-summary.md.

stages.name: Site preparation
stages.description: Grading, drainage, utility rough-in. 200 workers, 6 months.

stages.name: Structural frame
stages.description: Steel superstructure. The building takes shape. Hyperscale campuses routinely require 4,000–5,000 simultaneous workers at peak.

stages.name: Mechanical & electrical rough-in
stages.description: Conduit, ductwork, and primary cable runs installed before walls close. AI rack densities: 50–100 kW vs. traditional 5–10 kW.

stages.name: Power infrastructure
stages.description: Substations, switchgear, and distribution gear landed and connected. Transformers ordered 18+ months in advance.

stages.name: Cooling systems
stages.description: Chillers, cooling towers, precision air handlers commissioned. AI heat density demands direct liquid cooling rather than air.

stages.name: Network & fibre
stages.description: Inside plant fibre, cable management, and patch panels installed. AI hyperscale requires up to 36x more internal optical fibre than traditional data centres.

stages.name: Final commissioning
stages.description: Systems integration, load testing, sign-off. Blocked by missing long-lead components — see missingComponents.

missingComponents.name: Main power transformer
missingComponents.description: 128–144 week lead time (~3 years). Ordered 18 months ago. Still in production. Custom wound; one dominant supplier globally.

missingComponents.name: Medium-voltage switchgear
missingComponents.description: 45–100 week lead time. Factory delayed by a supplier's materials shortage. Dependent on specialised components from 2–3 global suppliers.

missingComponents.name: Specialised fibre optic cable
missingComponents.description: 60+ week lead time. Global preform shortage — the same crisis as every other data centre being built simultaneously. New preform capacity will not alleviate shortages before 2027.

missingComponents.name: Commissioning engineer
missingComponents.description: The one certified engineer for this switchgear model is booked 14 months out. No substitute exists. ~30% of active union electricians are aged 50–70; 439,000-worker construction deficit as of late 2025.
